sources.summary: The paper analyzes GN20 as a z=4.0548 dusty star-forming galaxy with extreme infrared luminosity, high star formation, massive stellar content, clumpy ionized gas, and rotationally dominated kinematics.

quotes.quote: We investigate the spatially resolved distribution and kinematics of the ionized gas in GN20, a dusty star forming galaxy at z =4.0548. We present deep MIRI/MRS integral field spectroscopy of the near-infrared rest-frame emission of GN20. The UV bright region of GN20 does not have any detected \ emission, suggesting an age of more than 10 Myrs for this region of the galaxy. From the rotation profile of \ we conclude that the gas kinematics are rotationally dominated and the v_ rot / _ m = 3.8 1.4 is similar to low-redshift LIRGs.

GN20 is a DSFG at redshift 4.0548 Carilli11 , located in a proto-cluster or galaxy overdensity Daddi09 and has an infrared luminosity of 1.86 10 ^ 13 L _ . The star formation rate derived from fitting the observed spectral energy distribution (SED), assuming a Chabrier initial mass function (IMF), adds up to 1860 \ Tan14 . With a total stellar mass of 1.1 10 ^ 11 \ Tan14 , GN20 is located well above the z =4 star formation main sequence Speagle14,Caputi17 . The specific star formation rate of GN20 computes to sSFR = 10^ 7.7 yr ^ 1 , just below the starburst line for z = 4 -- 5 derived by Caputi17 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies GN20 as a spectroscopically confirmed z~4 SMG, finds its photometric redshift consistent with spectroscopy, and notes a strong overdensity of nearby massive IRAC-selected galaxies tracing a proto-cluster.

quotes.quote: For two of these galaxies, GN20 and GN20.2, (objects 15541, 13857 in our sample) spectroscopic redshifts around z 4 were recently determined by hereafter D08 Daddietal2008 . Those objects are among the most distant SMGs spectroscopically confirmed known at the present. Our photometric redshifts for these galaxies are consistent within the error ( 0.2 ) with the spectroscopic ones (being 3.75 and 3.95 for GN20 and GN20.2 respectively).

It is interesting to remark that D08 discuss the evidence of a galaxy proto-cluster at z 4 in the GN20 and GN20.2 area, due to the finding of an over-density of B-dropout galaxies. Four objects in total from our z>3.5 sample ( 13857, 14722, 15268, 15541) are also within a 25'' radius centered on the coordinates of GN20. This represents an overdensity of a factor of 18 with respect to the expected number density of IRAC-selected galaxies in the field. Massive galaxies seem to be tracing this proto-cluster structure at z=4.05 .

sources.summary: The paper uses MIRI/JWST imaging to resolve GN20 stellar structure, finding a luminous central source plus envelope, offsets from UV/dust emission, and merger-driven starburst growth.

quotes.quote: The 5.6 m image taken with the JWST Mid-Infrared Instrument (MIRI/JWST) shows that GN20 is a very luminous galaxy (M _ 1.1 m, AB = 25.01, uncorrected for internal extinction), with a stellar structure composed of a conspicuous central source and an extended envelope. The central source is an unresolved nucleus that carries 9 of the total flux. The nucleus is co-aligned with the peak of the cold dust emission, and offset by 3.9 kpc from the ultraviolet stellar emission. The diffuse stellar envelope is similar in size (3.6 kpc effective radius) to the clumpy CO molecular gas distribution. The centroid of the stellar envelope is offset by 1 kpc from the unresolved nucleus, suggesting GN20 is involved in an interaction or merger event supported by its location as the brightest galaxy in a proto-cluster.

The stellar size of GN20 is larger by a factor of about 3 to 5 than known spheroids, disks, and irregulars at z 4, while its size and low S ersic index are similar to those measured in dusty, infrared luminous galaxies at redshift 2 of the same mass ( 10 ^ 11 M _ ). GN20 has all the ingredients necessary for evolving into a massive spheroidal quiescent galaxy at intermediate redshift: it is a large, luminous galaxy at z = 4.05 involved in a short and massive starburst centred in the stellar nucleus and extended over the entire galaxy, out to radii of 4 kpc, and likely induced by the interaction or merger with a member of the proto-cluster.

sources.summary: The paper treats GN20 as a z=4.05 starburst in GOODS-N, deriving high dust mass, low CO conversion factor, high SFE, and a starburst-mode interpretation.

quotes.quote: We explore the gas-to-dust mass ratio ( ) and the CO luminosity-to- conversion factor ( ) of two well studied galaxies in the GOODS-N field, that are expected to have different star forming modes, the starburst GN20 at z=4.05 and the normal star-forming galaxy BzK-21000 at z=1.52 . Detailed sampling is available for their Rayleigh-Jeans emission via ground based mm interferometry ( 1.1-6.6 mm) along with PACS and SPIRE data that probe the peak of their infrared emission.

Using the physically motivated Draine Li (2007) models, as well as a modified black body function, we measure the dust mass ( ) of the sources and find (2.0^ +0.7 _ 0.6 10^ 9 ) for GN20 and (8.6^ +0.6 _ 0.9 10^ 8 ) for BzK-21000. The addition of mm data reduces the uncertainties of the derived by a factor of 2 , allowing the use of the local vs metallicity relation to place constraints on the values of the two sources.

For GN20 we derive a conversion factor of < 1.0 pc ^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 , consistent with that of local ULIRGs, while for BzK-21000 we find a considerably higher value, 4.0 pc ^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 , in agreement with an independent kinematic derivation reported previously. The implied star formation efficiency is 25 L _ /M _ for BzK-21000, a factor of 5-10 lower than that of GN20.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution EVLA CO imaging of GN20, finding a massive rotating molecular-gas disk with multiple kpc-scale clumps and evidence against a simple major-merger interpretation.

quotes.quote: By comparing different models to the data, we find that the best--fit model is a rotating disk with an inclination of i = 30 ^ 15 ^ , a maximum rotational velocity of v_ max = 575 100 km s ^ 1 , and a dispersion of = 100 30 km s ^ 1 (Figure fig:GN20_mom1 , right). While the resolution of the data make it difficult to constrain the exact shape of the rotation curve, we find that the velocity field is fully consistent with a rotating disk with a steeply--rising rotation curve that quickly flattens. We derive a dynamical mass for GN20 of 5.4 2.4 10 ^ 11 M _ . We will use this estimate to set limits on the CO--to--H _ 2 mass conversion factor in the following.

The high--quality, high--resolution data on GN20 presented in this work reveal an extended gas reservoir, 14 4 kpc in diameter, which is consistent with a rotating disk. The data further resolve the reservoir into multiple, kpc--sized gas clumps which each constitute a few percent of the total gas mass and together comprise only 30 of the total gas mass. These observations may favor a scenario where the star formation is fueled by a process other than a major merger, e.g., cold mode accretion.

sources.summary: The paper treats GN20 as a confused high-redshift SMG with deblended Herschel photometry and retains it in an AGN subsample after subtracting a best-fit near-infrared power-law component.

quotes.quote: M12 note that one of these (GN15) is isolated and so its measured flux densities should be reliable, however for the other three (GN5, GN20, GN20.2) the FIR emission is confused with that from near neighbours, which may lead to some overestimation of their FIR fluxes. For GN20 we use the Herschel photometry from Magdis11 , which has been carefully deblended based on 24 m and radio positional priors.

We find 6/34 galaxies are classed as AGN in this way (AzLOCK.01, AzLOCK.10, AzTECJ100019+023206, LOCK850.04, LOCK850.15 and GN20 Although the observed S_ 8.0 /S_ 4.5 colour traces the rest-frame 1.6 m stellar bump at z 4 , we retain GN20 in our AGN sample as Riechers13 found that GN20 has an obscured AGN from power-law emission in the rest-frame MIR spectrum. ). Following the method of Hainline11 , we subtract a power-law with f_ ^ , where =2 or 3, from all photometry shortwards of 8 m (observed), incrementally adjusting the power-law contribution at 8 m to achieve the best-fit. In the following results we use the best-fit power-law subtracted values for the four AGN with weak power law components (AzLOCK.1, AzLOCK.10, LOCK850.15 and GN20). We use the results derived using a power-law slope of =3 as this provides the best-fit to the data, except for the case of GN20 where the data is best-fit by a power-law slope of =2 .

sources.summary: The paper describes GN20 as a gas-rich, hyper-luminous SMG in a z=4.05 pair and protocluster, with compact overdensity morphology.

quotes.quote: GN20 and GN20.2a were discovered as bright distant SMGs by Daddi09 . They have similarly high gas masses M_ H_2 =(0.9-1.5) 10^ 11 M_ and FIR luminosities, L_ FIR /L_ =13.20-13.46 , typical of HyLIRGs e.g., RowanRobinson2000,Neri2020 , which are often associated with infall/outflows, mergers, and mutual interactions. They are thus possibly interacting as indeed they are separated by just 24 arcsec (i.e., 167 kpc) in projection, while being at the same redshift z=4.05 .

Furthermore, Daddi09 found a strong overdensity of B-band dropouts and IRAC (Infrared Array Camera on the Spitzer Space Telescope) -selected massive z>3.5 galaxies, appearing to be centered on the two SMGs GN20 and GN20.2a, which suggests the presence of a z=4.05 protocluster. For B19 we have eight detected overdensities around the SMGs, including GN20, GN10, and possibly HDF850.1 at the highest redshifts z 4-5 , even though for the last one the association of the SMG with the overdensity is uncertain and not reported in Fig. fig:redshift_histo_PPMoverdensities . In some cases (e.g., GN10, GN20, and GN20.2a) they have compact morphologies, similarly to CL J1001+0220 at z=2.51 in the COSMOS field Wang16 .

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST/NIRSpec data for GN20, finding clumpy extended H emission, turbulent rotating gas with non-circular motions, and broad central emission consistent with AGN-driven winds or an active black hole.

quotes.quote: We present rest-frame optical data of the z 4 sub-millimeter galaxy GN20 obtained with JWST /NIRSpec in integral field spectroscopy (IFS) mode. The H emission is asymmetric and clumpy and extends over a projected distance of more than 15 kpc. To first order, the large-scale ionised gas kinematics are consistent with a turbulent ( 90 km/s), rotating disc ( v_ rot 500 km/s), congruent with previous studies of its molecular and ionised gas kinematics. However, we also find clear evidence for non-circular motions in the H kinematics.

In the centre of GN20, we find broad line emission (FWHM 1000-2000 km/s) in the H + [ N ii ] complex, suggestive of fast, AGN-driven winds or, alternatively, of the broad-line region of an active black hole. Elevated values of [ N ii ] 6583 /H >0.4 and EW(H )>6 throughout large parts of GN20 suggest that feedback from the active black hole is able to photo-ionise the interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper presents GN20 as a bright z~4 SMG: an extended gas-rich disc with clumps, unlensed, too bright for the model's main-sequence expectations, and likely rapidly fragmenting.

quotes.quote: As an example of what can potentially be learned from detailed follow-up of the sources that are potentially brighter than the proposed cutoff, we consider the case of GN20 Pope:2006 , which is an especially interesting and well-studied source. Recent high-resolution interferometric observations performed with the Very Large Array (VLA) indicate that GN20 is a very extended ( 14 kpc in diameter) gas-rich disc that contains at least five molecular gas clumps, each of which contain a few percent of the total molecular gas mass of the galaxy Hodge:2012 . In our model, the redshift evolution of the SFR-- relation alone cannot account for the extremely high flux density of GN20 (which does not seem to be lensed; Carilli:2010 ): Fig. fig:max_mstar_vs_z indicates that at z 4 , a galaxy with 5 10^ 11 would have S_ 870 9 mJy, and the inferred value for GN20 ( 2 10^ 11 ; Daddi:2009smgb ) is less than this value. Thus, GN20 seems to be an outlier from the main sequence see also Daddi:2009smgb,Carilli:2010,Carilli:2011,Hodge:2012 : its SSFR is 8.6 Magdis:2011 , but in our model, a z 4 main sequence galaxy with = 2 10^ 11 has SSFR = 2.3 , 4 less than that of GN20. The simplest interpretation of GN20 is that it is an extremely massive, gas-rich, gravitationally unstable disc undergoing rapid fragmentation, but whether such galaxies constitute a significant fraction of the SMG population is unclear.

sources.summary: The paper describes GN20 as a z=4.05 SMG with extensive FIR photometry; grey-body SED fitting gives Td=42.5 K and an optically thick solution, while Chandra non-detections argue against GN10-like X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The first high redshift SMG, GN20, was identified at z = 4.05 through observations of the CO [ 4 3 ] transition using the Plateau de Bure interferometer daddi2009b .

GN20 in our sample have 8 photometry measurement in FIR arranging from 100 to , providing a better constrain on the optical depth in the grey-body Planck function model. We fit the grey-body function to the SED of GN20 with both , _0 and T_ d as free parameters. The best fit yields = 1.9, T_ d = 42.5 K and _0 = 101 m.
Our best fit yields _0 = 101 m, thus _ 100 1, confirming that the optically thick assumption is valid for GN20.

sources.summary: The paper uses GN20 as a known GOODS-N SMG test case, finding the correct multiwavelength counterpart with a neural-network score of 0.7 while rejecting nearby non-SMGs.

quotes.quote: To test our results from an astronomical perspective, we checked a couple of well-known SMGs in the GOODS-N field, specifically the galaxies known as GN20 pope2005 and HDF850.1 hughes1998 . With the former, we were able to locate six multi-wavelength sources within the SCUBA-2 beam (four of them with no missing features), and we knew a prior which one of these sources corresponded to the actual submm source. Predicting with the ensemble algorithm, we were able to classify the correct multiwavelength source with a prediction score of 0.7, while the other galaxies in the beam were all correctly classified as non-SMGs. In particular, we found one non-SMG source within the beam area that had similar features compared to the real GN20 source. Although this source seemed similar by eye, the NN accurately classified it as a non-SMG, with a prediction score of 0.15, well within our 0.5 cutoff.

sources.summary: The paper finds GN20 has high SFR, high sSFR, disc-like near-IR structure, an offset stellar nucleus, and signs consistent with gravitational interaction.

quotes.quote: In particular, we observe that GN20 dominates the total SFR with a value 2500 while GN20.2b has the highest stellar mass ( 2 11 ). The two DSFGs classified as LTGs (GN20 and GN20.2a) have high specific SFR (sSFR > 30 Gyr ^ 1 ) placing them above the star-forming main sequence (SFMS) at z 4 by 0.5 dex, while the ETG (i.e., GN20.2b) is compatible with the high-mass end of the main sequence.

This implies that if GN20 is a disc-like galaxy, assuming q_0 = 0.2, it has an inclination i = 38 2 , in agreement with the value derived from its CO(2-1) emission (i.e. i = 30 15 , Hodge+12 ). We also find an offset between the centroid of the extended stellar emission and the nucleus of 1 kpc (see Fig. fig:lenstronomy ), in agreement with Colina+23 . This offset is similar to those observed in local galaxies in clusters Lauer+88 , predicted to be the result of tidal interactions Aguilar&White+86 , suggesting that GN20 could have been part of a gravitational interaction.

sources.summary: The paper says GN20 has an unambiguous interferometric counterpart, extended radio emission, multi-band detections, and B-dropout colors implying high redshift.

quotes.quote: GN20: The counterpart to this submm source is now unambiguously known, due to detections with both IRAM and the SMA (Pope et al. in preparation; Iono et al. 2006). The new radio reduction has also revealed a source at 1.4 GHz which looks extended. This counterpart is detected in MIPS, IRAC and ACS. The source is a B dropout, which implies that it is at high redshift, and we are currently obtaining optical spectroscopy to confirm this.

gmw_00000566.name: NGC 5584
gmw_00000566.overview: NGC 5584 is a flocculent spiral galaxy featuring a small, bright nucleus and star formation associated with its spiral arms [arXiv:1205.1936]. The galaxy hosts a population of Cepheid variables [arXiv:2102.12489, arXiv:2307.15806] and contains cold, low-density molecular gas clouds in its outer disk [arXiv:1910.04596].

sources.summary: The paper independently reanalyzes HST Cepheids in the SNIa host NGC 5584, deriving a Cepheid distance, comparing Milky Way and LMC calibrations, and assessing SH0ES agreement.

quotes.quote: The Cepheids in the type Ia supernova (SNIa) host galaxy NGC 5584 played a key role in the local measurement of H_0 . The SH0ES project used the observations of this galaxy to derive a relation between Cepheids' periods and ratios of their amplitudes in different optical bands of the Hubble Space Telescope (HST), and used these relations to analyse the light curves of the Cepheids in around half of the current sample of local SNIa host galaxies. In this work, we present an independent detailed analysis of the Cepheids in NGC 5584.
The Distance to NGC 5584 In this section, we derive the distance modulus of NGC 5584, based on apparent Wesenheit magnitudes W_H of our sample of 82 Cepheids in this galaxy.

We measure two distance moduli for NGC 5584 using two different PL relations calibrated in MW and LMC. The result from the former is in agreement with the value from SHOES within 1 , and the result from the latter is 0.147 0.060 mag smaller than that of SH0ES, though still within 2.5 . We do not attempt at reporting a value for H_0 based on the distance to only one SNIa host galaxy, and we only note that a smaller distance to NGC 5584 points towards a higher H_0 value. However, we consider the MW PL relation to be more appropriate for distance measurements to NGC 5584, due to similar metallicity and structure of these two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper observes Cepheids in NGC 5584 with JWST, derives a distance modulus from NGC 4258 calibration, and finds robust agreement with HST-based results.

quotes.quote: We present JWST NIRCAM observations in two epochs and three filters of > 320 Cepheids in NGC 4258 (which has a 1.5 maser-based geometric distance) and in NGC 5584 (host of SN Ia 2007af), near the median distance of the SH0ES HST SN Ia host sample and with the best leverage among them to detect such a bias. JWST provides far superior source separation from line-of-sight companions than HST in the NIR to largely negate confusion or crowding noise at these wavelengths, where extinction is minimal. The result is a remarkable > 2.5 reduction in the dispersion of the Cepheid relations, from 0.45 to 0.17 mag, improving individual Cepheid precision from 20 to 7 . Two-epoch photometry confirmed identifications, tested JWST photometric stability, and constrained Cepheid phases. The relation intercepts are in very good agreement, with differences (JWST HST) of 0.00 0.03 and 0.02 0.03 mag for NGC 4258 and NGC 5584, respectively.

For NGC 5584, the mean background bias (i.e., crowding correction) derived from the artificial stars is 0.03, 0.07 and 0.08 mag and the random errors due to crowding (derived from the dispersions of the individual sets of artificial stars) are 0.08, 0.13, 0.21 mag in F090W, F150W, and F277W, respectively. The values are very similar for NGC 4258, 0.03, 0.06 and 0.08 for the background bias and 0.07, 0.13, 0.20 for the random errors. The similarity of these values means the bias has negligible impact on the distance determination for the SN Ia host; forgoing crowding corrections altogether for both hosts increases the net distance measured to the SN Ia host by 0.008 mag.

We derived a distance of NGC 5584 from the two step ladder by adding the geometric distance modulus for NGC 4258, _ 0,N4258 =29.398 mag Reid:2019 to the intercept difference between NGC 5584 and NGC 4258, i.e., _ 0,N5584 =m_H^ W,N5584 m_H^ W,N4258 + _ 0,4258 . where m_H^ W,N5584 and m_H^ W,N4258 are the intercepts of their hosts linear relations. Our baseline distance modulus to NGC 5584 is _0=31.813 0.020 mag (not including the geometric distance uncertainty for NGC 4258 of 0.032 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5584 as a massive BADGRS with HST Cepheid metallicities, weak narrow CO lines in the outer disk, and low R_31 ratios in quiescent disk regions.

quotes.quote: For NGC 5584, we use the comprehensive set of measurements as part of the HST Cepheid study by Riess2011 which results in 14 metallicity measures across the three pointings.

In particular, the most massive galaxy, NGC 5584, has very narrow lines in the outer regions of the disk (FWHM 20-30 ). Since the regions probed by the \ beam are 2--3 kpc in diameter, such narrow lines indicate that the CO is likely to be in small clouds with narrow intrinsic line-widths.

The lowest ratios ( 0.2 < R_ 31 < 0.3 ) are in the outer disk regions (O2) of the two most massive galaxies, NGC 5584 and NGC 5496. At face value this low ratio indicates very cold ( 10-20 K) and/or low density ( n 1000-2000 cm^ 3 ) clouds similar to those found in the quiescent disk regions of external galaxies Zhu2009,Wilson2009 , however, the R_ 21 values at these locations are relatively high.

sources.summary: NGC 5584 is described as a flocculent bulgeless galaxy with a small bright nucleus, spiral-pattern star formation, and diffuse extended disk radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4701 and NGC 5584 : Both galaxies have a small bright nucleus and a flocculent spiral structure. Star formation is associated with the spiral pattern. The NVSS images show extended but diffuse radio emission from the disks and is probably associated with star forming regions over the spiral arms. NGC 4701 also shows some emission in the higher resolution FIRST radio map. The emission is concentrated about the nucleus and the inner disk.

gmw_00000567.name: DDO 154
gmw_00000567.overview: DDO 154 is a gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Canes Venatici I Group [arXiv:2006.01735, arXiv:2006.01735]. The galaxy features an extended, lopsided neutral hydrogen disk with a warp in its outer regions [arXiv:1501.06424, arXiv:0810.2116] and is currently undergoing a high rate of star formation [arXiv:1807.07093].

sources.summary: The paper uses DDO 154 as a prototype dwarf whose earlier rotation curves implied strong dark-matter domination, but argues beam smearing likely underestimated its inner slope.

quotes.quote: Rotation curve studies of the dwarf galaxy DDO 154 (Carignan Freeman 1988; Carignan Beaulieu 1989) indicated, however, that this galaxy is dominated by dark matter at all radii, including the region well within the optical disk. Even when the contribution of the stellar disk is scaled as high as is allowed by the observed rotation curve (i.e., the maximum disk solution), the stellar disk could not be scaled to explain the observed rotation curves out to two or three disk scale lengths. The observations of DDO 154, along with studies of scaling relations based on relatively few well-studied dwarf galaxies (e.g., Casertano van Gorkom 1991; Broeils 1992a; Persic et al. \ 1996), led to the generally accepted picture that dwarf galaxies have slowly rising rotation curves and are dominated by dark matter at all radii.

However, it is useful to consider as an example the well-studied prototype dwarf galaxy DDO 154. From \ synthesis observations of this galaxy a slowly rising rotation curve was derived, and mass modeling indicated that DDO 154 was almost entirely dominated by dark matter (Carignan Freeman 1988; Carignan Beaulieu 1989). Based on the published mass model, we estimate that =0.52 , much lower than the values found for our sample. Those early observations had a resolution of 45'' , and the derivation of the rotation curve was certainly affected by beam smearing, especially given the galaxy's inclination of approximately 66^ (de Blok \ 2008). Recently, de Blok \ (2008) presented higher resolution \ data for this galaxy.

sources.summary: For DDO 154, the paper adopts published distance, photometry, and rotation information, notes consistent R-band measurements, and emphasizes that extended observations show a lopsided outer distribution with declining rotation.

quotes.quote: DDO 154 A description of distances, photometry, and rotation can be found in the paper of Carignan Beaulieu (1989). The distance of 3.8 Mpc is based on a combination of brightest star considerations and association with the Canes Venaticorum I cloud. Photometry is available in the B,V, and R bands. An additional measurement in the R-band by Swaters Balcells (2002) is consistent, both concerning the profile and total luminosity.

The rotation curve of Carignan Beaulieu exhibits a decline beyond a radius of 300 . In a following study by Carignan Purton (1998, hereafter CP98) additional observations reveal a more extended low level structure. The outer distribution is lopsided but the velocity field appears rather regular. Again the derived rotation curve starts to decline abruptly and continues so out to a radius beyond 540 .

sources.summary: The paper uses DDO 154 as the only suitable THINGS analog for testing pressure-support corrections, finding only weak pressure support and minor changes to its inferred rotation and density profile.

quotes.quote: The remaining galaxies are DDO 154 ( V_ max 50 ) and NGC 2366 ( V_ max 60 ), both of which are in a regime where the effects of pressure support may be marginally detectable. However, of these, NGC 2366 has a strongly declining rotation curve, suggestive of a kinematically disturbed galaxy, leaving DDO 154 as the only candidate for providing a legitimate analog of the models discussed in this paper. The few galaxies with potentially lower rotation speeds are nearly face-on, have asymmetric velocity fields, or are nearly spatially unresolved. The rotation curve of DDO 154 was kindly given to us in electronic format by Erwin de Blok, based on deblok2008 .

In Figure ddo154fig we plot the resulting data for DDO 154. The right hand panel shows the velocity dispersion profile (dotted line), the observed tangential velocity (black solid line), and the inferred underlying circular velocity (red), both for the observed gas surface density (solid line) and for an exponential surface density extrapolated to the center (dashed lines). There is little evidence that pressure support plays any significant role in this particular galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper measures DDO 154 pattern speeds across most of its disk, finding similar outer-disk models, central or outer H I corotation, and sensitivity to adopted position angle.

quotes.quote: Results for DDO 154 Similar to NGC 3031, different regions: |y| 400 , |y| 200 , 400 y 0 , and 0 y 400 , produce consistent results. Therefore, only the results for most of the disk, |y| 400 , are presented. This region provides 24 independent calculations of Equation (1).

All three models of _p are quite similar in the outer half of the disk. The plot for AM1 differs from the other two by showing corotation in the central part of the galaxy. From the results for the F test, the results of the KS test for the constant _p model, and the plots of _p , the clumps of H I in DDO 154 are corotating in the central and outer parts of the disk. Some of the clumps of H I at 100 ^ are rotating more slowly than the material.

sources.summary: The paper presents DDO 154 as the EBHIS test case, fitting its integrated spectrum and finding total H I flux consistent with prior combined interferometer and single-dish measurements.

quotes.quote: Results Results are presented here for only a single galaxy, DDO 154. Figure fig_R shows the spectrum of DDO 154 obtained by integrating a 9 9 pixel area of the relevant data cube. The profile shown is the mean of 10^4 iterations of a converged MCMC, but it is almost indistinguishable to the eye from the best-fit profile from a Levenberg-Marquardt optimisation.

A Levenberg-Marquardt optimization returned a best-fit value of 102 4 Jy km s ^ 1 for the total flux of DDO 154, whereas the MCMC returned a mean value of 100 5 Jy km s ^ 1 . These values are significantly higher than the value of 82 fitted to the data of walter_2008 . However, it is known that interferometric measurements can miss flux. Our value agrees very well with the value of 105 5 Jy km s ^ 1 reported by carignan_1998 from a careful combination of interferometer and single-dish data.

sources.summary: The paper identifies DDO 154 as a significant outlier with an unusually high H I gas fraction, suggesting it may be a rare late-merger dwarf that has not yet consumed excess gas.

quotes.quote: As discussed in sec:mstarmrot , our \ relation in Figure fig:carina_plot has one significant outlier, DDO 154. This galaxy also has an unusually high HI gas mass fraction, with M_ HI /M_* = 37 . At its currently observed star formation rate of M _* = 3.82 10^ 3 2012AJ....143...47Z , DDO 154 would move onto our \ relation in 5.7 Gyrs. This is an interesting timescale.

In CDM, most major galaxy mergers are complete by redshift z=1 some 8 Gyrs ago e.g. 2009ApJ...702.1005S . Thus, if post-merger isolated dwarfs look like DDO 154, then most would have had time to deplete their excess HI gas and move onto the \ relation by today. A possible explanation for DDO 154, then, is that it has just undergone a relatively rare late merger. We will explore this idea further in future work.

sources.summary: The paper uses DDO 154 as an archetypal gas-rich low surface brightness dwarf with high mass-to-light ratio, and finds such extreme dwarfs are not separated from normal dwarfs in M--SFR space.

quotes.quote: The first such examples were dwarf irregular galaxies with extended \ envelopes; DDO 154 serves as the archetype of this class of gas-rich LSB dwarfs KrummBurstein . At the other end of the mass scale, the serendipitous discovery of Malin 1 BothunEtAl, ImpeyBothun , a very massive, dominated LSB disk galaxy, provided an example of the class of ``crouching giants'' predicted by Disney . A common feature of Malin 1 and DDO 154 is their high \ mass:light ratios: M / L_B 5 [ compared to M / L_B 0.1 --1 for `normal' detected LSB galaxies FisherTully, Waugh ] . In addition to the observational problem of determining their abundance --- which PASS has begun to address --- galaxies like DDO 154 and Malin 1 pose the following theoretical problem: ``Why was there so little gas processed into stars'' in these systems CarignanBeaulieu ? Are these objects exceptional in the way in which they are presently forming stars?

Moreover, in the range M 10^8 -- 10^ 10 M , it seems that the majority of dwarfs are forming stars in the same way: `extreme' objects like DDO 154 and ESO215-G?009 are not distinguished from `normal' dwarfs in the M --SFR plane. In response to the question ``Why was so little gas processed into stars?", therefore, these galaxies' high \ mass:light ratios are not due to inefficiencies in the present mode of SF. Instead, they may be, for example, a reflection of late formation times or differing environmental effects.

sources.summary: The paper compares DDO 154 with NGC 3109 as nearby dwarf galaxies whose resolved rotation curves favor similar low-cusp, zero-stellar-mass best-fit kinematic models.

quotes.quote: In order to contrast the results for NGC 247 with dwarf galaxies, we have applied the same analysis to DDO 154 and NGC 3109. These galaxies, observed by Carignan Beaulieu (1989) and Jobin Carignan (1990) respectively, were shown by Moore (1994) to be inconsistent with a dark matter halo that follows a Hernquist (1990) profile. Both galaxies are nearby and have been observed with good spatial resolution (see Table tab:data ). For NGC 3109 R _1 = 5.2 and R _2 = 27.8 such that it has one of the best resolved HI velocity fields. While DDO 154 has only R _1 0.7 , the radius where the rotation curve changes slope is significantly larger than the beam size.
The best fit kinematic models of both galaxies are remarkably similar. Both have = 0 ( 0.5 at the 99.73 percent confidence level), 12 and _B = 0 .

sources.summary: The paper uses DDO 154 as the prototype fast-rotating dwarf, emphasizing its excessive H I linewidth, low luminosity for the Tully-Fisher relation, and gas-rich baryonic correction problem.

quotes.quote: The first sample consists of rotationally supported galaxies, and has DDO 154 as a prototype. They have HI linewidths significantly larger than predicted by an extrapolation of the Tully-Fisher relation. The deviation from the extrapolated Tully-Fisher relation correlates with the distance-independent mass-to-lumi no sity ratio M_ HI /L_ B . The baryonic correction for these gas rich galaxies is too small to explain the deviation from the Tully-Fisher relation, unless a small mass-to-light ratio (M/L_ B )_* 0.25 for the stellar population is assumed.

Such a large deviation from the T-F relation was, in fact, first noticed by Carignan Beaulieu (1989) for the dwarf galaxy DDO 154 and later by Meurer et al. (1996) for NGC 2915. Both objects have been included in the present sample.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for DDO 154, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: DDO 154 DDO 154 (Fig. fig:ddo-154 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] DDO 154 is a quiescent, gas-rich dwarf galaxy with a warp in the outer parts. It does not have a compact, well-defined center in the 3.6 m IRAC image, or in the radio continuum. We attempted to derive a center position by fitting ellipses at a few representative intensity levels to the IRAC image, and the total \ map. The resulting central positions showed a variation of up to 15 , demonstrating that deriving an unambiguous photometric center for DDO 154 is not straightforward.

sources.summary: The paper says DDO 154 has regular, solid-body-like rotation without significant non-circular motions, and reports a BEC rotation curve and mass estimate for the galaxy.

quotes.quote: DDO 154: The rotational velocity curve of the galaxy DDO 154 shows a regular rotation pattern. No significant non-circular motions can be observed in the galaxy. The rotation curve resembles that of a galaxy with solid-body rotation, with a steep increase in the inner regions. The BEC rotation curve is obtained by taking R=7.5 kpc and _c=6 10^ 25 g/cm ^3 , which gives a mass of M_ DDO154 ^ BEC =4.71 10^ 9 M_ . The mass of the galaxy determined from observations is M=5.40 10^9M_ .

sources.summary: The paper fits DDO 154 with a slightly larger distance, notes the flat outer rotation-curve points may reflect a gaseous-disk warp, and finds razor-thin gas disks fit poorly.

quotes.quote: In the case of DDO 154 this is 1.085 times the measured distance of 4.3 Mpc and as such is 4.67 Mpc. It is not possible to fit the inner curve and outer flat data points beyond 7 kpc with the same model. The flat outer points may be due to a warp in the outer part of its gaseous disk ( carpur98 ). Nevertheless, the solid and dashed lines achieve excellent fits up to 6 kpc and the dashed line uses an acceptable distance and gas scale-height of only 0.65 kpc. The dotted line uses a razor thin gas disk and is a poor fit to the inner curve and poorer still to the outer curve. claim that dwarf galaxies (such as DDO 154) have thicker H I disks than more massive spiral galaxies due to a lower restoring force from weaker gravity.

sources.summary: The paper highlights DDO 154 as a dark-matter-dominated dwarf galaxy where MDM and CDM models predict especially different stellar escape speeds.

quotes.quote: However, the largest differences in stellar escape speeds between both models are predicted for dark matter dominated dwarf galaxies such as DDO 154, and kinematical studies of such galaxies could prove key in establishing, or abolishing, the validity of the MDM model.

We then construct in Section 3 CDM models for the galaxies NGC 2403, DDO 170 and DDO 154 (whose rotational curves have previously been modelled using MDM), and calculate and compare escape speeds from the stellar disc as a function of galactic radius for both types of model. We show that the MDM models predict specific values for the escape speeds that are generally different from those in the CDM models.

As in the BPR model, we use data from , , and for the intermediate spiral galaxy NGC 2403 and the dwarf galaxies DDO 170 and DDO 154. Again assuming a constant mass-to-light ratio for each galaxy and taking M_ L and r_ 1,d as fixed parameters, we fit the rotational curves of these three disc galaxies by using , v_ max and r_ s as fit parameters.

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO 154 as a nearby gas-dominated dwarf whose outer rotation curve is overpredicted by MOND without an external field, motivating an EFE fit.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension and declination of 12^h54^m05.2^s and 27^d08^m59^s

quotes.quote: (1) DDO 154 : DDO 154 is a gas-dominated nearby dwarf galaxy with right ascension and declination of 12^h54^m05.2^s and 27^d08^m59^s , respectively. It is located at 3.84 Mpc from us (with the maximum and minimum distance of 4.3 and 3.02 Mpc, respectively ddo-dm, ddo-md ). The luminosity of the galaxy is about L_V=4.40 ( 0.92) 10^7 L_ ddo-lu . The measured very low-internal acceleration is in the deep-MOND regime (i.e., a_ internal a_0 ) and it is free of various uncertainties that occur at higher accelerations. Due to the overestimation of rotational velocities in the outer regions of DDO 154, Milgromian dynamics without the EFE fails to describe the observed rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO 154 as a late-type dwarf irregular with an extended HI disk, rotational support, and a dense compact dark-matter halo suppressing small-scale spiral structure.

quotes.quote: We select five late-type dwarf irregular galaxies, namely, DDO 154, NGC 3741, DDO 43, NGC 2366 and DDO 168 in which we study the constraining influence of dominant dark halo on the formation of small-scale spiral arms. Two of these (DDO 154, NGC 2366) are a part of the THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Wal08 , whereas DDO 43 and DDO 168 are a part of the LITTLE (Local Irregulars That Trace Luminosity Extremes) THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Hun12 . Our sample galaxies have the following characteristics: These have a large HI disk (extending to several optical radii), and in the inner regions as well as in the outer regions the larger part of the baryonic contribution comes from HI gas, and not stars. The galaxies are rotationally supported. We find that for these low luminosity galaxies, the corresponding dark matter halo is dense and compact, i.e., the central density ( _0 ) is few times 10^ 2 M _ pc ^ 2 and the core radius (R _ ) is less than two-three times the exponential disk scalelength (R _ d ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies DDO 154 as a significant outlier with high current star formation and gas fraction, possibly after a recent major merger.

quotes.quote: Finally, there is one more significant outlier in the \ relation (Figure fig:SFR-M200-plot , right panel): DDO 154 (marked D154). Uniquely amongst the dIrrs that we consider here, DDO 154 is currently forming stars at four times its \ averaged over a Hubble time 2012AJ....143...47Z . It also has an unusually high HI gas fraction of M_ HI /M_* = 37 2017MNRAS.467.2019R . As noted by 2017MNRAS.467.2019R , at its currently observed star formation rate of M _* = 4.3 10^ 3 M _ yr ^ 1 2012AJ....143...47Z , DDO 154 would move onto the M_*-M_ 200 relation in 5.7 Gyrs. This may indicate that it has recently undergone a major merger that increased both its M_ 200 and its SFR, but has not yet increased its M_* .

sources.summary: DDO 154 is used as a representative dwarf galaxy to compare HI velocity-dispersion estimates and hydrostatic solutions, showing an extended HI disc sensitive to halo modeling.

quotes.quote: To compare how our derived _ HI compares with other estimates, we evaluate the _ HI profile in a representative galaxy DDO 154 using two other different methods. First, adopting a similar approach as tamburro09 , we calculate the _ HI by averaging the observed MOMNT2 in annular rings, as denoted by the magenta asterisks in Fig. sigcomp . Secondly, we use the derived _ HI profile in DDO 154 by iorio17 through a rigorous 3D tilted ring fitting using the software ^ 3D Barolo diteodoro15 (black squares).

We also find that the  disc in DDO 154 at 1.5 kpc extends to much higher heights ( 750 pc) as compared to the stellar disc. An extent of the  disc to a height of 750 pc turns out to be much higher than what is observed, for example, in the Milky Way (few hundred parsecs at R=10 kpc kalberla07 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents DDO 154 as an excellent test case whose flat axisymmetric flow is tightly constrained and whose rotation curve has little wiggle room.

quotes.quote: DDO 154 This dwarf galaxy is extremely well fitted by our flat, axisymmetric disc model, since the velocity residuals, Figure  fig.DDO154 , are generally small. The covariance plots in the bottom panel of the same figure reveal that each global parameter of the best fit model, red symbols, is near the centre of the distribution of values from the 200 bootstrap iterations, and the projection geometry is tightly constrained. Strong degeneracies between the location of the centre, the inclination, and systemic velocity can arise when fitting velocity maps of dwarf galaxies, but in this case they are mild, with just weak correlations in the expected sense between V_ sys and the (x,y) position of the centre.

We find that the data on DDO 154 are extremely well fitted by a simple, flat axisymmetric flow pattern having tightly constrained projection parameters and that dB08 in fact overestimated the uncertainties in the fitted circular speed. On the other hand, we could not find a credible fit to the data from NGC 2366. Therefore we would argue that DDO 154 provides an excellent test case for comparison with galaxy formation models, but that nothing useful could be learned from including NGC 2366 in such a study.

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO154 as the barred irregular in its rotation-curve sample and says the flat-disc model fails there, with inconsistent results caused by the model's simplicity.

quotes.quote: Moreover, the results are clearly inconsistent for the irregular barred galaxy DDO154 . These three cases suffer, in fact, from the simplicity of the model: these galaxies, and mainly the DDO154 , are poorly approximated by a flat disc.

For the irregular barred galaxy DDO154 , the approximation is simply invalid. Hence, even the fact that the results for this last case is inconsistent with the other cases seems to be a positive aspect of the model exploited here, due to the simplicity of the modelization made.

sources.summary: The paper models DDO 154 as a THINGS dwarf irregular, deriving its H I vertical scale height and finding strong flaring linked to a centrally concentrated dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: We note that, with the exception of Ho II which has a thick gas disk throughout, the dwarfs all show H i flaring. The rate of flaring is highest in NGC 2366 and DDO 154 which can be ascribed to these two galaxies having a more centrally concentrated dark matter halo than the other two systems. Figure fig3 presents a collection of plots of the logarithm of the scale height versus radius, showing a near exponential behaviour, i.e. a linear slope, in each case. The curves for DDO 154 and IC 2574 display a break, necessitating two different slopes across two radial regimes, i.e., the gas scale height increases exponentially with radius with different scale lengths across the two regimes.

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS low-surface-brightness galaxy in Group A, finding fitted BDM cores with r_s exceeding r_c, typical transition energy near 0.11 eV, and BDM fits preferred over other cored profiles.

quotes.quote: We obtain seven galaxies (DDO 154 (A1), NGC 2841 (A2), NGC 3031 (A3), NGC 3621 (A4), NGC 4736 (A5), NGC 6946 (A6), and NGC 7793 (A7)) (we call Group A) with fitted values r_s > r_c 0 that are shown in Table tab:onlydm_bdm . For these galaxies we obtain average values for: the core r_c 40 pc , r_s 5 kpc , and the energy E_c 0.11 \ eV for the min.disk analysis, that are typical values for a galaxy, except for DDO 154 which has a larger core, r_c = 1.35 kpc . We notice that for all these galaxies the ^2 in BDM is smaller than NFW's but the extra parameter ( r_c ) in BDM makes the reduced ^2_ red equivalent for both profiles. When comparing the BDM profile with the other two cored profiles, Burkert and ISO (Ref. ) we conclude that BDM is still better.

sources.summary: The paper compares DDO154 with the fiducial simulated dwarf, emphasizing similar maximum circular velocity, rotation-curve behavior, stellar and gas velocities, and central dark-matter density.

quotes.quote: In Table rotcurvetable we directly compare our fiducial dwarf galaxy with full radiative feedback with two galaxies from Oh11a , NGC2366 and DDO154, which have similar maximum circular velocities to our models. The circular velocity profile of the fiducial model with full stellar radiation feedback is a very good match to rotation curves of observed galaxies of similar mass, NGC2366 and DDO154, except perhaps in the central 1 , where the rise in the circular velocity of the fiducial run is slightly steeper.
For DDO154 they find maximum values v_ circ 7 for the stars, and v_ circ 20 for neutral atomic Hydrogen.
For instance, the dark matter density of NGC2366 at a distance of 500 pc is _ DM 0.04 pc ^ 3 , while for DDO154 the density at the same radius is 0.05 pc ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies DDO 154 as the strongest individual HAWC dwarf-irregular target, compares its limit with the combined dIrr result above and below 10 TeV, and relates its annihilation limits to dSph constraints.

quotes.quote: We can observe that galaxy DDO 154 gives the best limit because of its position in a declination band where HAWC has good sensitivity.

We can observe that the combined limit shows two behaviors: for energies < 10 TeV, the combined limit is the same or even worse than the DDO 154 limit; for energies > 10 TeV, the combined limit is better than DDO 154 limit.The combined limit is not better than the DDO 154 limit below 10 TeV because of the large dispersion in the values of the signal-to-background ratio of dIrr galaxies.

For annihilation of dark matter (Figure anaComp ), we observe that the combined limit for dIrr galaxies (solid black line) and the DDO 154 limit (solid red line) are comparable to the exclusion limits obtained for classical dSphs, but not to limits for ultra-faint galaxies which have large values of J-factor in comparison to dIrr galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 154 as a Canes Venatici I Group galaxy with nine detected holes and one of the largest LITTLE THINGS disk sizes.

quotes.quote: DDO 154 DDO 154, also known as PGC 43869, UGC 8204 and NGC 4789A, is 3.7 Mpc away from us with a V-band magnitude of 14.2 in the Canes Venatici I Group . An earlier study done by found two holes and found nine holes. We also detected nine holes with the majority being of Type 1. Among LITTLE THINGS galaxies, DDO 154 has one of the largest disk sizes ( R_ max = 7.1 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes DDO 154 as a THINGS disk galaxy with a discontinuous outer disk geometry and velocity differences tied to varying inclination.

quotes.quote: DDO 154 The TRM analysis shows a clear discontinuity in the outer disk at R 2 R_ 25 200'' , with a large variation of 30^ in the inclination angle. The P.A. is relatively constant, but with some fluctuations in the very inner regions (as seen in Fig. DDO154 ). As a result of this, the transversal velocity ( v_t ) is similar to the circular velocity ( v_c ) with a slight difference in amplitude due to the varying inclination angle in the outer disk.

sources.summary: DDO154 is used as a SPARC downward-hook example whose non-monotonic total acceleration and monotonic baryonic profile can make the RAR slope change sign inward.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:sparc_hooks shows RAR tracks for three examples (DDO154, NGC0055 and UGC06667), chosen to represent the diversity of hook behaviour in the SPARC database, and the aggregate RAR for all SPARC galaxies (grey 2D histogram).

For example, DDO154 and NGC0055 both have non-monotonic \ profiles accompanied by monotonic \ profiles. As can be seen from inspecting Equation eq:rarscale , such a situation can naturally produce downward hooks, where the value of m = (p_ tot 2)/(p_ bar 2) changes from positive to negative. As the radius decreases, \ peaks ( p_ tot = 2 and m=0 ) and then begins to decline ( p_ tot >2 , m<0 ) whilst \ continues to rise ( p_ bar < 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 154 as an extremely gas-rich dwarf with a large asymmetric gaseous disk, clear non-circular motions, and rotation-curve analysis still pending.

quotes.quote: DDO 154, an extremely gas-rich dwarf galaxy at 3.2 Mpc distance, is known to have a large gaseous disk and a very asymmetric rotation curve, which appears to decline in the outer parts (e.g.
DDO 154 exhibits clear signs of non-circular motions.
The derivation of the rotation curve of DDO 154 has to await the final analysis with TiRiFiC.

sources.summary: DDO 154 is discussed as a dark-matter-dominated dwarf/low-surface-brightness THINGS galaxy whose Einasto halo matches simulated dwarfs in density-radius space but has a lower-than-expected index.

quotes.quote: Another interesting point concerns the results for the few dwarf and low surface brightness galaxies DDO 154 and NGC 2366. Due to their dark matter dominance they are ideal candidates for a direct comparison with CDM low mass halos ( 10^ 9 \ h^ 1 ). They perfectly agree with simulated dwarf galaxy halos in the density-radius parameter space (the two closest symbols to the red triangles in Figs fig:haloparam1 and fig:haloparam2 ) but they have a low Einasto index, contrary to all simulated dwarf-galaxy-sized halos.

sources.summary: The paper identifies DDO 154 as a dwarf and least massive sample galaxy, says asymmetric drift is negligible, and links its SFR scale height to HI because CO is undetected.

quotes.quote: In our sample, there are six normal spirals and six low-mass galaxies, whose circular velocity do not exceed 150 (DDO 154 is a dwarf galaxy).

However, 2017Iorio showed that its contribution is negligible in the case of DDO 154, which is the least massive galaxy in our sample. , IC 2574, and NGC 7793 were not included in 2016Frank sample as no CO emission was detected, for these we used the mass models reported in .
Clearly, in the case of DDO 154, IC 2574, and NGC 7793, h_ SFR (R) coincides with h_ HI as CO emission is not detected.

sources.summary: The paper identifies DDO 154 as a dwarf galaxy with less extended star formation than H I and notes the model cannot fully reproduce its observed energy.

quotes.quote: For DDO 154, NGC 2403, NGC 3198, and NGC 6946, _ SFR (R) is less radially extended than _ HI (R) , as the FUV emission from the outermost radii goes below the sensitivity limit of the observations.
Among our galaxies, only the dwarf galaxy DDO 154 is not included in this sample.

However, our model cannot fully reproduce the observed energy in the region 2 kpc R 5 kpc of DDO 154 and in the outskirts of IC 2574 and NGC 6946, indicating that some contribution from the thermal energy of the WNM may be required.

sources.summary: The paper studies the dwarf irregular galaxy 154, testing whether CDM halo simulations can fit its observed rotation curve without adding Burkert and Silk’s spheroidal MACHO component.

quotes.quote: We investigate the claim by Burkert and Silk BS97 that the observed rotation curve of the dwarf irregular galaxy 154 cannot be reconciled with the universal CDM halo profile of Navarro, Frenk White NFW96,NFW97 even when allowance is made for the effect of violent gas outflow events on the structure of the galaxy. By means of N body simulations we show that under certain conditions it is possible to obtain a reasonable fit to the observed rotation curve without invoking Burkert Silk's proposed spheroidal MACHO component. We are able to best reproduce the observed decline in the rotation curve by postulating additional hidden disc mass, in an amount that is compatible with disc stability requirements.

gmw_00000568.name: 2MASS J1051+1247
gmw_00000568.overview: 2MASS J1051+1247 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.2828 [arXiv:2003.08690]. Hubble Space Telescope observations of the active galactic nucleus reveal four energetic outflows with similar velocities and distances of a few hundred parsecs from the central source [arXiv:2003.08690].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST/COS observations of quasar 2MASS J1051+1247, identifying four similar energetic outflows whose kinetic power can substantially contribute to AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: Plesha Space Telescope Science Institute, 3700 San Martin Drive, Baltimore, MD 21218, USA We detect four very energetic outflows in the Hubble Space Telescope /Cosmic Origin Spectrograph spectra of quasar 2MASS J1051+1247 with a combined kinetic luminosity ( E K ) of 10 ^ 46 erg s ^ 1 . Remarkable similarities are seen in these outflows: velocity centroids between 4900 and 5700 km s ^ 1 , distances from the central source ( R ) of a few hundred parsecs that are all consistent within the errors, and an E K within a factor of two for all outflows.

2MASS J1051+1247 (J2000: R.A. = 10:51:25.69, decl. = +12:47:46.2, z = 1.2828) was first observed by HST /COS in 2013 May (PID 12603) and again in 2018 January (PID 14777). Table tab:obs contains the details of each observation.

Collectively, the outflows have a kinetic luminosity of 8.8 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , which is 7.0 6.5 2.3 of L edd . Therefore, these outflows, on average, carry enough energy to contribute substantially to AGN feedback in galaxies with similar black hole masses as in quasar 2MASS J1051+1247.

gmw_00000569.name: NGC 4631
gmw_00000569.overview: NGC 4631 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:0905.3995, arXiv:1401.2719] undergoing active starburst activity [arXiv:0909.2658, arXiv:1610.02404] and strong gravitational interactions with its neighbor NGC 4656 [arXiv:1112.1300, arXiv:astro-ph/0610114]. These interactions have produced extensive tidal structures [arXiv:2301.00937], a large stellar stream [arXiv:1410.6368], and a highly extended radio halo [arXiv:1112.1300, arXiv:2210.07709].

sources.summary: The paper presents LOFAR imaging of the edge-on galaxy NGC4631, targeting its known extended radio halo and finding an extended synchrotron halo comparable to earlier low-frequency images.

quotes.quote: 3C286 and the edge-on galaxy NGC4631 were also simultaneously observed in May 2011 during nighttime. The purpose of observing this galaxy was to detect the extended radio halo known to exist seen in Golla Hummel (1994). The large thickness of the radio halo is due to the high star formation efficiency rate in the disk as well as gravitational interaction with NGC4656 which can be seen from Krause (this volume).

Figure 1, right image, shows an image of NGC4631 that has been calibrated with BBS on a single source in the field itself (4C+32.40) with a total flux of 3.2 Jy (at 157 MHz), interpolated between NVSS and VLSS. This was then followed by two selfcal cycles. The rms noise is 20mJy/beam which again can be improved within a subband. The beam is 165 118 arcsecs. A very extended synchrotron halo can be seen which is comparable to the image from Hummel Dettmar (1990) at 327 MHz.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4631 rotation-measure reversals in its northern halo, finding rotation-only models poor, outflow models reasonable, and accretion best for the modeled region.

quotes.quote: Rotation measure (RM) synthesis maps of NGC 4631 show remarkable sign reversals with distance from the minor axis in the northern halo of the galaxy on kpc scales. To explain this new phenomenon, we solve the dynamo equations under the assumption of scale invariance and search for rotating logarithmic spiral solutions. Solutions for velocity fields representing accretion onto the disk, outflow from the disk, and rotation-only in the disk are found that produce RM with reversing signs viewed edge-on. Model RM maps are created for a variety of input parameters using a Faraday screen and are scaled to the same amplitude as the observational maps.

Solutions for rotation-only, i.e. relative to a pattern uniform rotation, did in general, not fit the observations of NGC 4631 well. However, outflow models did provide a reasonable fit to the magnetic field. The best results for the specific region that was modelled in the northern halo are found with accretion. Since there is abundant evidence for both winds and accretion in NGC 4631, this modelling technique has the potential to distinguish between the dominant flows in galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4631 as an edge-on galaxy whose low-frequency radio halo is exceptionally extended, with high thermal contribution in its disk.

quotes.quote: With newly reduced radio continuum data of five edge on galaxies (NGC 891, NGC 3432, NGC 4013, NGC 4157, and NGC 4631), we plan to set new constraints on the morphology of radio halos and the physical properties of galactic winds driven by stellar feedback. By distinguishing between the central and outer regions of the galaxies, our study setup allows us to search for variations in the radio halo profile or CR transport along the Galactic disk.

While the radio halo of NGC 4013 shows only a small extent in z direction, the radio halos of NGC 891, NGC 3432, and NGC 4157 reach already far up into the galactic halo. The most extreme case in this sample is certainly NGC 4631. Here, the extent in z direction almost exceeds the galaxy's major axis.

In L-band the thermal emission constitutes 30 of the overall observed radio emission in the galactic disk (for NGC 4631, the thermal contribution reaches even up to 40 in the galactic disk). However, the thermal fraction in the HBA data is much smaller. Here, the thermal emission normally does not exceed 10 of the total emission (only NGC 4631 shows some regions where the thermal fraction reaches 15 ).

sources.summary: The paper maps the interacting Whale galaxy NGC 4631 with FAST, finding an extended diffuse HI reservoir, tidal structures, active star formation, and gas accretion linked to interactions.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 4631, known as the Whale galaxy, is an edge-on spiral galaxy, and has remarkable tidal structures Weliachew78,Rand94 . It is centered at _ 2000 =190.9905 , _ 2000 =32.1682 , according to the 2MASS Extended Source Catalog Jarrett00 . It has a heliocentric systematic velocity of 615 Rand94 . We take the error weighted mean of luminosity distances derived with the TRGB method in the literature Seth05, Tully13, Radburn-Smith11, Monachesi16 , which is 7.53 Mpc.

As the brightest galaxy of the N4631g, NGC 4631 has two major companions, NGC 4656 and NGC 4627, 70.5 and 5.6 kpc away in projected distance respectively, the interaction with which should have produced most of the tidal structures around NGC 4631. Its interaction with NGC 4656 might start only a few hundreds of million years ago, as suggested by the age of a tidal dwarf near NGC 4656 SchechtmanRook12 , and the simulation of Combes78 in an attempt to reproduce its tidal tails. It also has many other fainter dwarf companions, and stellar tidal tails which do not correspond to the tails MartnezDelgado15 . These properties indicate a dynamic, actively interacting environment around NGC 4631. NGC 4631 has an active star formation possibly due to the active tidal interaction with neighbors.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4631 as the likely interacting group member that stripped gas toward NGC 4656, producing bridges and a plausible 200-300 Myr origin for NGC 4656UV.

quotes.quote: NGC 4656UV is a member of the loose (total number of members 6; Garcia93 ) galaxy group consisting primarily of two edge-on spiral galaxies, NGC 4656 and NGC 4631, which are suspected to have had a strong interaction in the past. An \ bridge discovered by Roberts68 extends between the two spirals suggesting gas has been stripped from NGC 4631 by its gravitational encounter with NGC 4656.

We make the case that it is most likely a tidal dwarf galaxy created from an encounter between NGC 4656 and NGC 4631, instead of a pre-existing, but highly rejuvenated, dwarf galaxy, or a tidal stream.

The most likely culprit for this interaction is NGC 4631. The difference in the line-of-sight velocities towards NGC 4656 and NGC 4631 is 40 , which is extremely low for a group velocity dispersion, and may suggest that most of the relative velocity between the two galaxies is in the plane of the sky. With a projected separation of 36 kpc and assuming a typical group velocity dispersion of 200 , we estimate that the two spiral galaxies could have interacted as recently as 230 Myr ago, comparable to the post-starburst age estimates from the GALEV models. Detailed kinematic modeling is required to strengthen the case that NGC 4656UV was created by an interaction, but the \ bridge observed by Weliachew78 and Rand94 and lend weight to NGC 4631 as the perturber.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4631 as an interacting edge-on spiral with a large halo, deriving thermal radio fractions, halo scale heights, convective cosmic-ray escape, galactic wind, and magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: NGC 4631 is an interacting galaxy that exhibits one of the largest, gaseous halos observed among edge-on galaxies. NGC 4631 has a global thermal fraction at 5.99 (1.57) GHz of 14 3 (5.4 1.1 ). The total magnetic field of NGC 4631 has a mean strength of B_ eq 9 G in the disk, and a mean strength of B_ eq 7 G in the halo. From the halo scale heights we estimated that the radio halo is escape-dominated with convective cosmic ray propagation, and conclude that there is a galactic wind in the halo of NGC 4631.

The normalized scale height for the halo in NGC 4631 is 6.8 2.1 ( 6.4 1.0 ) at 5.99 (1.57) GHz, and perfectly fits into the linear anti-correlation of the normalized scale height with the mass surface density, as found by . We estimated that the radio halo is escape-dominated with convective cosmic ray propagation, and conclude that there is a galactic wind in the halo of NGC 4631. The total equipartition magnetic field strength of NGC 4631 using the synchrotron spectral index map is estimated to be B_ eq 9 G in the disk and B_ eq 7 G in the halo, which is somewhat smaller than values estimated previously assuming a constant spectral index.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes FUSE data for NGC 4631, finding halo O VI emission fields consistent with a warm-hot filament and noting the galaxy is interacting.

quotes.quote: Here we report four additional detections of emission gas in the halos of nearby edge-on galaxies, NGC 4631 and NGC 891, using archival Far Ultraviolet Spectroscopic Explorer data and an updated data pipeline. We find the most intense emission to be from fields forming a vertical line near the center of NGC 4631, despite the close proximity to the disk of two other fields. The detected emission surface brightness are about 1.1 0.3 10^ 18 to 3.9 0.8 10^ 18 . The spatial distribution of the five 30 30 detection fields in NGC 4631 can be interpreted as We theorize that the vertically-oriented fields trace a warm-hot phase gas filament that is possibly infalling onto the galaxy.

NGC 4631, also known as the Whale Galaxy, is a nearby edge-on barred spiral galaxy with companions NGC 4627 and NGC 4656 2018Richter . NGC 4631 has a distance of 7.4 0.2 Mpc, and, based on data, is interacting with several nearby systems 2011RadburnSmith . NGC 4631 was originally observed with because 1987SPIE..733..519P observations indicated a concentration of soft X-ray emission above the plane of the galaxy but correlated with star formation activity in the plane 2002fuse.prop.C057M .

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 4631 with the sample, finding exceptionally halo-like diffuse HI tied to intensive feedback, polar interactions, and extensive tidal structure.

quotes.quote: A question after comparing the sample studied here with NGC 4631 ( Wang23 ) is why NGC 4631 stands out? The HALOGAS data is an order of magnitude deeper in column density than the THINGS data, and their uv coverage on short baselines by design is much denser than the latter, yet they missed 40 ( > 70 ) of the fluxes detected by FEASTS in the NGC 4631 system (CGM region), the central galaxy of which is edge-on Wang23 .

NGC 4631 is indeed a special system for which even the HALOGAS observation misses a large fraction of in comparison to the FEASTS data. If it were observed by THINGS, the missed fraction would have been even higher, possibly higher than all the THINGS galaxies studied in this paper. The diffuse of NGC 4631 has distinct column densities ( 3 times higher), velocity dispersions ( 2.5 times higher), and inferred thicknesses ( 10 times higher) in comparison to all other galaxies studied here. Most of the diffuse is distributed perpendicular to instead of along the disk of NGC 4631, again in contrast to the sample here. NGC 4631 is indeed an extreme case in term of its halo properties, not only in diffuse , but also in radio continuum, H , X-ray, etc Martin01, Wang01, Irwin12 .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4631 at 870 m and 1.23 mm, deriving dust spectra and masses and finding plane, halo, ring-like, and outer-disk dust structures tied to its interacting system.

quotes.quote: We have observed the continuum emission of the interacting galaxy NGC 4631 at 870 m and 1.23 mm using the Heinrich-Hertz-Telescope on Mt. Graham and the IRAM 30-m telescope on Pico Veleta. We have obtained fully sampled maps which cover the optical emission out to a radius of about 7' at both wavelengths. For a detailed analysis, we carefully subtracted the line contributions and synchrotron and free-free emission from the data, which added up to 6 at 1.23 mm and 10 at 0.87 mm. We combined the flux densities with FIR data to obtain dust spectra and calculate dust temperatures, absorption cross sections, and masses.

The emission is concentrated on the galactic plane, although some halo emission could also be detected, especially at 1.23 mm where intergalactic cold dust seems to follow the previously detected H i spurs. The two maxima visible in both maps, located in the plane symmetrically around the nucleus at a radius of about 30'' , resemble a ring-like structure, similar to the distribution of the CO molecular line emission. In the outer disk, where no CO is detected, the mm/sub-mm emission follows the H i distribution, similar to other edge-on galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4631 radio polarization and finds a disk-parallel field, a regular halo magnetic field, large scale-height variations, and tidal-interaction links.

quotes.quote: Only NGC 4631 seems to have a different field orientation in its disk. Along the eastern and western halves of the disk of NGC 4631 the magnetic field orientation is parallel to the galactic plane, but in the central region of the disk a vertical field seems to dominate. In order to clarify whether NGC 4631 has a unique magnetic field configuration in the central region along its disk, we present high-resolution Faraday-corrected polarization data.

Our main conclusions are the following: We fit a single power law with the total flux-densities of NGC 4631 at four different frequencies and obtain a total integrated spectral index of _ tot = -0.78 0.04 . By subtracting the thermal fraction of the emission from the total flux-densities, we obtain a nonthermal integrated spectral index of _ nth = -0.87 0.03 . The vertical scale heights in NGC 4631 vary significantly in different regions within the galaxy. Their mean values at 4.85 GHz are with 2.3 kpc (370 pc) for the thick (thin) disk higher than the mean values found in six other edge-on spiral galaxies studied so far Krause2011 . There are several indications that convection may be strong in NGC 4631, which may originally be related to its tidal interaction with its neighbouring galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4631 as an edge-on spiral and brightest group member, with newly found low-surface-brightness companions and tidal structures toward NGC 4656.

quotes.quote: According to b11 , a group of galaxies around NGC 4631 accounts 28 members with a wide range of absolute magnitudes M_B from 20^m till 10^m . The group is characterized by the mean radial velocity of +635 km/s in the rest frame of the Local Group, the velocity dispersion of 90 km/s, and the mean harmonic radius of 243 kpc. The total stellar mass of the group members amounts to (M_*/M_ ) = 11.12, and the virial (projected) mass of the group is equal to (M_p/M_ ) =12.98 , what is 72 times the stellar mass. The brightest group member is a spiral late-type galaxy NGC 4631 seen nearly edge-on.

Radburg-Smith et al. (2011) determined the distance to NGC 4631 as 7.38 Mpc, via the Tip of Red Giant Branch using the ACS camera aboard the Hubble Space Telescope. With this distance, NGC 4631 has a linear Holmberg diameter of 33.7 kpc and an absolute magnitude of M_B=-20 28 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4631 as a well-studied edge-on CHANG-ES galaxy with H imaging and Effelsberg-corrected radio data for thermal-component separation tests.

quotes.quote: Hence, this study will examine the validity of predicting the thermal emission in edge-on galaxies using both ancillary H and mid-IR imaging as a proxy for thermal emission in a subsample of 3 galaxies from the CHANG-ES survey: NGC 891, NGC 3044, and NGC 4631, and aim to identify a consistent method of thermal prediction for edge-on galaxies. Calibrated radio continuum images and methodology from Ph.D. theses schmidt and mora are used in this study for NGC 891 and NGC 4631, respectively. We identified three galaxies within the CHANG-ES survey with pre-existing and readily available H imaging of their entire disks, in order to test methods of independently estimating the thermal radio component using both H and Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE; wrightetal10 ) 22 micron mid-infrared imaging. The three galaxies chosen for this test sample were NGC 891, NGC 3044, and NGC 4631. In addition to the current availability of H imaging for these galaxies, NGC 891 and NGC 4631 are both well-studied.
The radio data used for NGC 891 and NGC 4631 are corrected for short spacings using single-dish Effelsberg 100-m telescope observations, taken with the purpose of supplementing the CHANG-ES data.

sources.summary: The paper reports ten confirmed NGC 4631 satellites, rejects a projected compact system as background, and links NGC 4627 physically to NGC 4631.

quotes.quote: NGC 4631 Table tab:ngc4631_sbf shows the SBF analysis results for NGC 4631. There are 10 confirmed satellites, and no candidates that are possible/unconfirmed. dw1242p3231 is an exception to our usual criteria. It has strong SBF signal that is consistent with being at the distance of NGC 4631. However, it is a small compact system that is projected onto the outskirts of the disk of NGC 4631.
We do not attempt an SBF measurement for NGC 4627, but it is clear this galaxy is physically associated to NGC 4631 both from redshift and ongoing tidal disruption.

sources.summary: The paper applies its log-normal temperature model to NGC 4631, reproducing Chandra spectral fits and arguing that the model matches existing observations beyond simple 1-T/2-T treatments.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We take the RA and Dec as 142.8^ and +84.2^ , respectively. The choice of the RA and Dec is arbitrary but it so happens that these coordinates correspond to those of NGC 4631 .

quotes.quote: To illustrate the applicability of the log-normal model to existing data, we discuss the Chandra spectrum of NGC 4631 . and discuss the Chandra spectrum for NGC 4631 and fit it using the 2T model at 3 10^6 and 7 10^6 K, having luminosities equal to 9.3 10^ 38 and 13.7 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. Figure 3 of shows that the 2T model can fit the data quite well.

We produce the Chandra mock spectrum from such a distribution for 60 ks observation period and the relevant parameters for NGC 4631 . We find that the best-fit peak temperature is 5 10^6 K with a corresponding =0.4 , and we show the best-fit log-normal spectrum in Figure fig:logn_chandra (red dashed line). We note that log-normal spectrum matches the observed spectrum well.

sources.summary: The paper fits broadband and X-ray data for edge-on NGC 4631, using inclination-dependent attenuation and X-rays to improve constraints on its recent star formation rate.

quotes.quote: To demonstrate how X-rays emitted from the stellar binary population can be used to help constrain the SFR for an inclined galaxy (since X-rays are less sensitive to dust attenuation), we fit the global broadband photometry of the edge-on nearby galaxy, NGC 4631. For the UV-to-submillimeter photometry, we utilized the 30-band SINGS/KINGFISH data presented in Table 2 of 2017ApJ...837...90D . We then corrected the data for Galactic extinction before fitting, using the E(B - V) values in Table 1 and A_V normalized extinction values in Table 2 of 2017ApJ...837...90D . For the X-ray photometry, we made use of the Chandra ACIS-I data for a single 58 ks observation (ObsID 797).

Since we know that NGC 4631 is an edge-on galaxy, we set a tabulated prior on i generated from the Monte Carlo method described in Section 3 of 2021ApJ...923...26D , which converts an axis ratio into a distribution of inclination. The axis ratio and its uncertainty were retrieved from HyperLeda, which provides the axis ratio calclated from the 25 mag arcsec ^ 2 B band isophote.

sources.summary: The paper compares its Parker-instability magnetic-field reversals with NGC 4631, where alternating Faraday-depth regions and localized vertical magnetic fields are observed in the galaxy halo.

quotes.quote: The spatial pattern of the large-scale magnetic field may explain the alternating magnetic field directions in the halo of the edge-on galaxy NGC 4631. Our model is unique in producing a large-scale magnetic structure similar to such observation.

An arguably similar pattern of regions with the sign of the Faraday depth alternating along the direction perpendicular to the disc plane is observed in the edge-on galaxy NGC 4631 N4631 . The comparison of Figs fig:bxbyxy e--f and fig:bxbyxy -d shows that the Parker instability in a dynamo active system can produce rather complicated magnetic field structures. Our use of horizontal averages in Fig. fig:bxbyxy and elsewhere in the text conceals strong localised vertical magnetic fields typical of the magnetic buoyancy (see, e.g., Fig. fig:3d_vi_simb ), also observed in NGC 4631. Because of the low gas density at kpc-scale distances from the galactic midplane, observations of the Faraday rotation produced there are difficult; the observations of N4631 are the first of this kind, and future observation should show how widespread are such complex patterns.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4631 as a nearby edge-on SBc/d spiral hosting the ultraluminous supersoft X-ray source, and notes previous studies of its diffuse X-ray corona and point-source population.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a 4 h modulation for an ultraluminous SSS in the nearby edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 4631, observed with in 2002 June. In this Letter, we report the discovery of an even more luminous SSS with a slightly shorter modulation, located in the nearby edge-on galaxy NGC 4631 . NGC 4631 is a SBc/d type galaxy located at an assumed distance of 7.5 Mpc Golla1994 , with low galactic foreground absorption N_ H =1.2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ; Dickey1990 .
A study of the giant diffuse X-ray emitting corona around the galaxy was performed by with and by with . The point-source population viewed by was studied by and .

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 4631 with other X-shaped-field edge-on galaxies, attributing its crossing polarization vectors and rigid rotation to behavior the cosmic-ray dynamo may explain.

quotes.quote: One case (NGC 4631) exhibits different magnetic field orientation with field lines crossing the disk plane. This galaxy also is different when the rotation curve is taken into consideration. It rotates rigidly to the large radii vauc63 . The lack of differential rotation favors development of vertical structures at all galactic altitudes and reduces the speed of transformation of the radial component of magnetic field into the azimuthal one . We suggest that the peculiar structure of the polarized emission which is observed in NGC 4631, where vectors cross the disk plane, can result from the cosmic-ray driven dynamo.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4631 as an edge-on interacting spiral whose polarized emission is X-shaped, mainly extraplanar, brighter northward, and associated with the northeastern HI-extension region.

quotes.quote: NGC 4631 In this edge-on interacting spiral, the polarized emission is found in a roughly X-shaped morphology, and comes mainly from the extraplanar regions. The disk itself seems to be largely depolarized. Polarized emission is detected in the central region and in each of the four extraplanar galaxy quadrants. The north side is brighter in polarization than the south side. The brightest polarized intensity is from the northeast quadrant, which is the region where the dramatic HI extension studied by rand_1994 is located.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4631 as an intense starburst and very edge-on system whose geometry likely boosts the observed TIR-to-FUV ratio relative to a face-on view.

quotes.quote: Since we are interested in obtaining a fit valid for normal star-forming spirals, we have excluded three galaxies with intense starburst activity (NGC 4536, NGC 4631 and NGC 5713). In Fig. irxbeta a, only a few data-points follow the starbursts relation, and they indeed correspond to galaxies with intense star formation activity (e.g . NGC 4536, NGC 4631).

In very edge-on systems like NGC 4631 (which also happens to host starburst activity), geometry might also play an important role. The observed TIR-to-FUV ratio is probably larger than the one we would measure if the galaxy was face-on (besides the fact that this galaxy is probably more dust-rich than the average spiral galaxy). However, we do not find any significant trend between the position in the IRX- diagram and inclination, apart from this extreme case.
